stateKeys.name: dunningAttempts
stateKeys.type: integer 0..2 — retries burned against the current unpaid invoice

stateKeys.name: hasPaymentMethod
stateKeys.type: boolean — a chargeable payment method is on file

stateKeys.name: cents
stateKeys.type: integer >= 0 — the recurring amount last settled or agreed

specialRules.name: not-collectable
specialRules.note: Billing stimuli against a terminal subscription are observable rejections — Stripe keeps delivering webhooks for records that have already ended.

specialRules.name: first-invoice-still-open
specialRules.note: A failure on the FIRST invoice does not enter dunning; the subscription stays incomplete until it settles or expires.

specialRules.name: already-unpaid
specialRules.note: The dunning budget is already exhausted; further failures change nothing observable.

specialRules.name: already-terminal
specialRules.note: Cancelling an ended subscription is a no-op, not an error.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply in the current state is reject(reason) — an observable no-op, never a throw. Webhooks are redelivered at least once, so every stale or duplicate delivery must be a named rejection.
projectionBound: These four keys are the ENTIRE observable state. Proration, tax, invoice line items, payment-method fingerprints, coupons/discounts, currency, customer metadata and schedule/phase data are OUTSIDE the projection: not checked by any gate, not migrated, not audited. Every guarantee made about this machine is relative to this list.
